questions.content: Shirley wants to research nuclear energy because
options: A the fuels used in nuclear energy release radiation even after they have been used
B she wants to know how nuclear waste is disposed of
C of public concerns over nuclear power stations situated close to where they live

questions.content: Regarding other energy sources Richard
options: A is going to research renewable energy sources
B is not going to research renewable energy sources
C has not decided if he is going to research renewable energy sources

questions.content: For the next meeting Shirley will find some information about
options: A the benefits of coal and oil

sections.instruction: Complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Although we cannot do anything about the plants and animals that are already {{BLANK}} we can do something about the many species in the world today that are in danger of becoming extinct in the future.

The main reason for the growing number of {{BLANK}} species is the destruction of their {{BLANK}} The rapidly {{BLANK}} human population needs more land and resources for {{BLANK}} This is changing and destroying the habitats that plant and animal species need for {{BLANK}}

One way we can save endangered species and permanently {{BLANK}} these habitats is to create and support national parks and reserves. National parks encourage people to enjoy the {{BLANK}} of species without harming them.

People who visit these parks must be sure to obey few rules. These include following fire regulations, leaving {{BLANK}} placing all rubbish in a bin, not picking the flowers and not taking birds’ eggs.

Public {{BLANK}} will help ensure the survival of our plant and animal species.
